topic.name: Veterans' Health and Compensation
topic.description: Legislation and programs aimed at improving health care access and compensation for veterans with service-related disabilities.

Treats the VA as a participating provider for purposes of allowing the VA to recover the costs it incurs in providing care to a veteran for a non-service connected disability.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Exploration Policies
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the exploration, development, and leasing of oil and gas resources in federal and offshore areas.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and economically feasible and prudent transportation of oil and gas
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a competitive oil and gas leasing program for oil and gas exploration.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revoke existing presidential authority to withdraw any unleased lands
Authorizes a state governor to petition the Secretary of the Interior to issue leases for oil and natural gas exploration.
Document 4: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas
Instructs the Secretary to promulgate diligent development regulations.
Document 5: Allows any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) for which expenditure of federal funds for oil and gas leasing is prohibited
to be leased under the 2007-2012 oil and gas leasing program.
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil and gas lease for any submerged lands in the North Atlantic Planning Area

Document 7: Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing

Document 8: Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for oil and natural gas exploration

Document 9: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act of 2004 to include in the Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Project
any pipeline segment that the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission finds could feasibly be incorporated.
Document 10: Allows the governor of a state to petition the Secretary of the Interior to make a new producing area of the state eligible for oil leasing
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit 45% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids, and other sums due.

topic.name: Immigration Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to immigration, including provisions for deportation, visa categories, refugee status, and adjustments to residency for specific groups.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to report quarterly to Congress regarding each country that has refused or unreasonably delayed repatriation of an alien.
Prohibits the Secretary of State from issuing certain nonimmigrant visas to a citizen of a listed country.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category an alien coming temporarily to the United States to work as a sheepherder or dairy worker.
Provides for a three-year initial period of admission with additional three-year extensions.
Document 3: Provides for the permanent resident status adjustment of certain Liberian nationals who were granted, or are eligible to apply for, temporary protected status.
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust an alien's status from conditional resident to lawful permanent resident.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional permanent resident status, an alien who entered the United States before his or her 16th birthday.
Sets forth conditions for maintaining such status and having its conditional basis removed.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to carry out a Criminal Alien Program to identify incarcerated criminal aliens.
Makes a state that does not cooperate with the Secretary ineligible for Department of Justice (DOJ) law enforcement grant programs.
Document 8: Prohibits the U.S. refugee admission of an alien who is a national of, has habitually resided in, or is claiming refugee status due to events in any country containing terrorist-controlled territory.
The State Department shall maintain and continually update a list of the countries containing terrorist-controlled territory.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust the status of an eligible alien entrepreneur to that of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residency.
Requires that an eligible alien applies for such adjustment and is present in the United States on the date of the application's filing.
Document 10: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008, with respect to an unaccompanied alien child in federal custody.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) or the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to notify the governor of a state within 48 hours before the child's placement.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the accountability and assessment of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation, management, and preservation of wilderness areas and federal lands across various states.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas.
Document 2: This bill amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness.'
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Prohibits any use of motorized vehicles and other motorized machinery in the Area that was not permitted on March 1, 2006.
Document 4: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness.
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing, water rights, and Native American tribal rights.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to modify the contract to add three years to such contract's expiration date for timber sales from BLM lands.
Document 6: This bill releases areas in Colorado from further review for designation as wilderness.
Prohibits the inclusion of any portion of the Colorado River within the conservation area.
Document 7: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, and natural values.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
Document 8: Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Sets forth provisions concerning the management of federal lands in the Challis National Forest.
Document 9: Amends the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 to designate certain lands in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness.'
Prohibits anything in this Act from being construed as diminishing any right of any Indian tribe.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey four specified parcels of land in Twin Falls, Idaho, for public purposes.

Document 6: Requires a state plan for child protective services to include identification and assessment of children known or suspected to be victims of sex trafficking.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation

Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.

Document 2: Farm Relief Act of 2008 - Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act
emergency financial assistance to producers on a farm that incurred qualifying natural disaster-caused quantity or quality losses
Document 4: Farming Flexibility Act of 2011 - Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables
Document 6: Federal Milk Marketing Improvement Act of 2009 - Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act
base the minimum Class II milk price on the average production cost of producing all milk in the 48 contiguous states
Document 9: Wildfire and Drought Relief for Ranchers and Farmers Act of 2012 - Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Fuel Standards
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy sources, improving fuel standards, and integrating energy and water resource management.

Document 1: Requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to integrate water considerations into its energy research, development, and demonstration programs.
Advancing energy and energy efficiency technologies and practices that meet the objectives of minimizing freshwater withdrawal and consumption.
Document 2: Requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least 30% qualified vehicles in model year 2016.
Defines 'qualified vehicle' as a vehicle that operates on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by requiring the Administrator of the EPA to ensure transportation fuel contains at least the applicable volume of 'next generation biofuel.'
Defines 'next generation biofuel' to mean renewable fuel derived from cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin, or algae.
Document 6: Instructs the Director of the National Science Foundation to establish the Council on Renewable Energy to advise Congress on renewable energy development.
Promote renewable energy research and development in areas of wind, wave, solar, geothermal, and biofuels production.

topic.name: Data Breach Notification and Protection
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the requirements for notifying individuals and authorities in the event of data breaches.

Document 1: Amends federal law governing public printing and documents to instruct the Director of Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security.
Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify individuals within one hour after the discovery of a breach of personally identifiable information.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on all such breaches that occurred within the past year.
Document 4: Requires educational agencies or institutions to establish information security practices to protect students' education records and personally identifiable information.
Mandates breach notification policies to notify parents of breaches or security violations.
Document 5: Requires any federal agency or business entity to notify any U.S. resident whose information has been accessed or acquired following a security breach.
Notifications to be made without unreasonable delay following the discovery of a security breach.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security of any system maintained by a health care exchange.
Known to have resulted in the theft of or unlawful access to the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 8: Requires commercial entities to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data in electronic form containing personal information.
Directs a covered entity to notify individuals whose personal information was accessed and acquired by an unauthorized person.

topic.name: Taxation and Financial Crimes
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing taxpayer protections, combating tax fraud, and addressing money laundering and financial crimes.

Document 1: requires the IRS to submit to Congress a report including an assessment of notifying a taxpayer when a return has been filed
recommendations with respect to any infrastructure or other changes that would facilitate the notification process.
Document 2: establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions
increase the period for Internal Revenue Service review of tax returns involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions.
Document 5: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to take necessary action to correct a tax return affected by the misuse of a taxpayer's identity
authorize the Secretary to disclose tax return information to federal, state, and local law enforcement personnel.
Document 6: increase civil penalties for willful violations of anti-money laundering laws
impose a 20-year maximum prison term for individuals who facilitate evasion of an anti-money laundering program.
Document 8: expand prohibitions against conducting monetary transactions over $10,000 for the purpose of engaging in interstate criminal activities
authorize the Attorney General to obtain a 30-day ex parte court order freezing the bank account of any individual arrested or charged for crimes involving the movement of funds.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments and acts aimed at reforming and regulating financial institutions and their operations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency' that may share information with another covered agency.
Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving, destroying, or affecting any privilege.
Document 2: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs).
Limits such requirement to appearance of a fee disclosure on the ATM screen.
Document 3: Federal Reserve Accountability and Transparency Act of 2014 - Amends the Federal Reserve Act (FRA) to direct the Chairman of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) to submit to the Comptroller General (GAO).
Directs GAO to study whether the DPR has materially changed, audit any materially changed DPR, and report to certain congressional committees.
Document 4: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to exempt from specified Basel III regulations community banks with consolidated assets of $50 billion or less.
Document 5: Holding Company Registration Threshold Equalization Act of 2013 - Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) if its assets exceed $10 million.
Requires termination of such registration after a savings and loan holding company certifies that its holders of record of such class of security has been reduced to fewer than 1,200 persons.
Document 6: Financial Regulation Reform Act of 2008 - Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to subject credit-default swap trading clearinghouses to regulation by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
Establishes a temporary Commission on Financial Regulatory Reform to review the financial regulatory structure.
Document 7: Audit The Fed Act of 2013 - Repeals the authority of the Comptroller General (GAO) to carry out an onsite examination of an open insured bank.
Directs the Comptroller General to complete, before the end of 2014, an audit of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.
Document 8: FDIC Flexibility Act of 2008 - Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to repeal requirements for least-cost resolution of troubled insured depository institutions.
Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal limitations on advances to member banks by the Board of Governors.
Document 9: CLEAR Plus Act of 2015 - Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to subject a savings and loan holding company to registration requirements for securities.
The Federal Deposit Insurance Act is amended to require the federal banking agencies to issue regulations allowing for a reduced reporting requirement.
Document 10: Insurance Capital Standards Clarification Act of 2014 - Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements.
States that federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject any person to such minimum capital requirements.

to reduce traffic law violations at highway-rail grade crossings

methods that minimize the effects of gender bias in evaluation

topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative amendments related to retirement plans, pension benefits, and employee protections under ERISA and the Internal Revenue Code.

Document 2: Shrinking Emergency Account Losses Act of 2013 or the SEAL Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to loans made from a qualified employer plan.
